history.note: capture the entire onboarding flow both paths and capture screenshots

previousFix.description: This 'Choose plan error' is the same onboarding 'Still finishing your plan / Try again' screen as ddd1 — POST /onboarding/finalize was returning 500. Root cause (fixed last cycle): _active_diet_meal_count used .scalar() on the active-UserDietPlan query, which throws sqlalchemy MultipleResultsFound the moment a user has more than one active diet plan, crashing finalize and stranding the user on Try-again. Fix counts active meals over ALL active plan ids instead. Re-verified now: POST /onboarding/finalize returns 200 {onboarding_complete:true, has_diet:true, has_workout:true} for the account that was failing, and the dashboard loads past the gate (screenshot). Backend 3f1b629 (deployed + live).

issues.reviewReason: Plain version: after building the plan, the app sometimes hung on 'Still finishing your plan' even though the plan was actually saved fine. Cause: the app waited on a SECOND server check that lags a few seconds behind the save, instead of trusting the 'done' confirmation it already got from the save itself. Fixed (67ae35d8) — it now advances the moment the save confirms. Note: the released app still runs the old code, so you'll only see this gone after the next app release. Same root cause as 9da9.

issues.description: Create best UIUX for this pr section which looks good with the entire activity page and make sure you design some options before integration and choose the best by yourself don't ask me.

fix.description: Ran it live on /activity (your reply: 'run it and give screenshot'). The flat repeated 'best 20kg x10' PR list is now the swipeable 'Personal bests' carousel: trophy header + '6 this month' pill, one card per lift with the weight as the hero (Conventional Deadlift 140kg, Barbell Back Squat 120kg, ...), reps + how-long-ago underneath, third card peeking to signal horizontal swipe. Sits cleanly above Today's Workout / Pull Day A. Calm/tier-free per the design guardrails. Screenshot attached is the actual running build (commit 421c82dd).

previousFix.description: Designed options + picked the best myself, as asked. Before: a bare flat list (Strength - best X kg x Y - Nd ago) floating between the carded sections. After: a Personal bests CARD that matches the activity-page card rhythm - a calm header with an orange N

previousFix.description: 2nd-pass redesign per your note (design in cards, nice badass UI interactive and dynamic). The PR section is now a swipeable horizontal CAROUSEL of PR cards: each lift is its own card with the weight as a big hero number (140 kg, 120 kg...), an orange dumbbell domain accent + trophy header, snap-scroll (swipe for more), and a staggered entrance animation. Bold + dynamic, still on-brand (no XP bars / tier medals - calm-gamification guardrails). Same /strength/summary data. Frontend StrengthStrip.tsx (421c82dd); verified live on the activity page with seeded PRs (before/after attached). I picked this direction myself as asked. Visible in the app after the next release.

issues.reviewReason: Root cause FOUND (from your prod logs) + fixed. Your finalize calls today returned HTTP 200 - which the backend only returns AFTER it commits onboarding_complete AND verifies a real diet+workout (else 409) - yet you sat on Still

issues.description: When the build activity plan open the activity page directly with prefilled current workout in the entire page
issues.reviewReason: Traced it. 'Build Activity Plan' currently opens the FULL workout builder (/ai/create-workout-plan?focus=activity) and just scrolls to the Daily-activity section — so to actually reach the Activity page you have to build the whole 7-day workout first (the activity steps/cardio/NEAT targets are saved bundled WITH the workout; there's no standalone save). That's why you land in 'Build your workout' instead of on the Activity page. Note: routing here was deliberate — the older standalone AI activity chat-wizard (/ai/create-activity-plan) was intentionally bypassed in favor of this quick targets sheet. To make it land on /activity right after you set the targets (no full workout build), the cleanest fix is a small full-stack add: a standalone 'save activity targets onto the active plan' path, and for the focus=activity entry the Daily-activity sheet's primary CTA becomes 'Save & view activity' -> persists targets -> opens /activity prefilled. Want me to build that? Or should 'Build Activity Plan' instead just open /activity and let you edit the daily targets inline there? Either is doable — confirming the intended behavior before I build, since it changes a deliberate flow.
issues.reviewReply: yes build it and make sure we don't build the plan again but have the existing plan for exercise

issues.description: Calories overshoot then orignal proposed plan
issues.reviewReason: Dug into this with prod data. The diet plan has no stored 'target' — the kcal shown (2991) is the SUM of the assembled meals. On the test account the pipeline is CORRECT: computed target 1824 == assembled meals 1824 (TDEE 2189 minus deficit, floored at BMR), so I can't reproduce a 2991-style overshoot there. Most likely cause: the build wizard's PROPOSED target comes from the client calc (calcOnboardingStats) which has a goal-completion-DATE trajectory override (a steeper deficit to hit your target date), but the SAVED/displayed plan uses the server calc (calculate_target_calories) which is deficit-only with NO date logic. If a date was set, the proposed (lower) and the saved (higher) legitimately diverge -> looks like an overshoot. The builder also tolerates up to +10% over target before blocking. To fix the RIGHT one safely (this calc drives everyone's calorie goals) I need: the affected account's email, OR did you set a goal completion date on that build, OR the proposed number you saw vs the 2991? Once I know which target should win (date-driven trajectory or standard deficit), I'll reconcile the two calcs so the proposed and the saved plan always match.
issues.reviewReply: it was different during the onboarding
